notes:
Standard Light BNB:
Electric Thunder gives +39F on hit to keep pressure and is kind of safe with -3F on block
OD: +140 damage, +40F on hit, +4F on block, SA2/SA3 cancelable
Rolling Attack is more damaging and resets to neutral, but extremely unsafe if blocked
Beginner
Easy
Easy
Easy
Cannot start with 2LK -- Extra +100 damage from starting with 2LP can also be applied to other combos
Slightly more damage than the 2-hit version and gives extra time to buffer Rolling Attack.
Only +10 extra damage. Very sad damage scaling
Actually -190 damage. Damage scaling punishes Super Arts hard

3-Drive expenditure for little benefits
Only use this if you have no SA and this would be the killing blow
Just don't even try this. Go for a Basic Combo with SA1
If I have to present any data to convince you to not execute this combo, this guide has failed you

More so a string, has 2 frame traps and is safe on block. Punishes mashing in case it's blocked. Be aware of parries
214PP cancels into SA2/3
Beginner
If you hit confirm 4LP, re-route into this for extra damage, but less Oki
214PP has a very generous early cancel for 236236K, no need to time it

BNB route from 4MK. Electric is less damage but better to keep pressure
214PP is cancelable into SA2 for extended combo
These all can be done with 2MK instead of 5MK, but less damage
Never go for Rolling Attack without confirm, it is -23F on block
Rolling Attack has to be done off 1MK because of charge
Easy
Easy
Easy
Easy
Not worth it! Just go for the previous line and save 2 bars

2-Drive Route:
To land [2]8HK, begin holding the charge immediately after [4]6PP input.
If you opt to jump, do it in the charge direction to keep j.[4]6HP charged.
Hard
Easy

Corner combo from 4MK with some options for ending:
3HP for a easy hit
If you remembered to charge during 63214KK... [2]8HK does free extra damage
SA1 requires a 4F delay after 63214KK to not whiff
Medium
Medium
Medium
Requires at least 30F pre-charging
Normal Electric whiff at max. range. Use only if routing into SA2, otherwise go for Drive Rush Cancel instead
Amazing conversion value from a far away poke. Not worth it if poking with 2MK (3700 damage)
Not much to say about these, 2MK gives you a 20% penalty on anything that follows compared to 2MP
Beginner
Easy
Medium

Difficulty has been bumped for all of these because of low effectiveness and unsafe on block
For [4]6MP you gotta start charging even before confirming 6MK hits
Hard
Hard
Hard

Good cost-effective Drive Rush Cancel combo examples
Low hit route, your best damage for a 2MP/2MK combo
2MK really eats away on your potential damage
Medium
Medium

SA3 animation gives you 1,5 Drive bars back if not on burnout
This makes DR + SA3 options not as costly as they look when compared to OD combos
Medium
Medium

THE standard Heavy BNB. Often in longer combos where 5HP can be confirmed.
2PP~P has numerous potential follow-ups that can be utilized, including Blanka Doll setups, so become comfortable this route.
Just keep holding 2 on Coward Crouch to charge [2]8MK
If 5HP hit at max. range, [2]8MK is probably going to whiff, do [2]8LK instead (-80 damage)
Easy
Medium
Easy
Easy

These are not actually realistic to see use in a match, it is so hard to charge
Coming out of DR gives you enough charging time, but then Coward Crouch route just deals more damage
Also coming out of DR, you might not wanna spend 2 extra bars for the OD Ball route
Medium
Hard

Drive Rush Cancel is somewhat more worth in heavy combos spite of damage proration.
That said, it is hard to randomly hit a 5HP out of the blue.
Medium
Medium
Medium
Medium
The only moment that is going to be worth instantly burning yourself out like this is going for a K.O.
This. Maximum damage without any extra shenanigans.

Example of corner routes after guard crush.
In this case, Vertical Rolling Attack won't hit after 63214KK
Medium
Medium
Medium
Meterless/SA1 conversion from a Rainbow Ball cross-up.
3-Drive conversion from a Rainbow Ball cross-up.
Standard cross-up confirm for when hitting the opponent as high/early as possible.
Cross-up confirm when hitting close to the ground or on crouching opponents, allowing standard HP routing.

Drive Impact Starter examples. Can tack on whatever ender you'd like w/ the right amount of meter, including SA2 if you choose to OD Electric.

Wild Lift has numerous follow-up options and setups, so experiment.
Punish combo extension from your standard Blanka Ball. 1-Drive for an extra 700 damage is great value, so get used to using this on reaction to a punish counter.

Light punish combo examples. 1st is consistent at all ranges, the other two utilize drive for consistency and juiced damage, great for many attacks which are -4/-6. Application will require matchup knowledge.

-7 punish that happens to be your standard drive rush extension. As HK is only -2 and has a large animation for hit-confirming, having this as a hit-confirm alongside 5HK,5LP is pretty useful should you occasionally toss out 5HK in your pressure.
Anti-Air counter-hit combo routes. Enemy is propelled upward after counter-hit, allowing some follow-ups even after a trade. Just a few examples of what's possible.
Overhead combo from Counter-hit and Punish Counter respectively.

Punishes for blocked reversals. Drive Rush can be used to guarantee the 5HP extension, but this will prorate the combo and cost drive meter which you may rather avoid.
[4]6PP route is easier and more flexible (Numerous capable routings after [4]6PP leaves them airborne) at the cost of 2-drive but forces side-swap.
Punish for blocked reversals near the corner. If the opponent is close to the corner, the dash is unnecessary, making the route much easier.

3-Drive for a reliable, on-demand whiff punish route with numerous options left open for you after Wild Lift. Worth learning.
+3-4k damage can be easily tacked on with SA1-3 use.

Similar to the above, but from 5LP/2LP which have above average range for Light Attacks.
Good for possible spacing traps such as after a blocked Electric.

An easier alternative from 5LP/2LP utilizing the standard light drive rush combo.
Use if you cannot confirm the Punish Counter as this still leads to pressure/damage.
Buffer super behind either MP for a quick and easy whiff punish requiring no Drive. Can also be used as a basic poke/counter-poke, but be careful as SA 1 and 3 are very punishable if blocked.
On the odd chance you have a charge ready when you whiff punish and really want to save 1 drive meter.

Your BNB. DR>6MK is an easy to confirm button due to its length and can be used as a meaty in some situations.
If it lands, opt for any 5HP routing you want.

Alternatively DR>4MK gives enormous frame advantage, making it a decent/safer option for extending pressure by simply looping into itself as shown in the combo below.

Must be point-blank to the opponent: Easier to confirm due to repetition, HAS TO be reversed or parried. Can frame trap.

No Hit-Confirm: Use drive to 214PP for +4F and continue pressure if blocked.
Hit Confirm: Use drive to extend a combo with a [4]6PP Route.
The easier route to learn and less input heavy option, can still chain after a block if opponent still holding high block.
Route with 5LK frame trap to utilize if you have a hard time confirming the overhead hit and suspect the opponent will mash after blocking.
Overhead Drive Rush BNB.
Low Drive Rush BNB to mix in with your overhead. You'll want to be fairly close when landing the initial hit;
A Blanka Doll confirm similar to the first, with increased damage and extra time to confirm or extend pressure if blocked.
